title: US Treasury freezes $344 million in cryptocurrency linked to Iran's regime
summary: The Trump administration froze $344 million in cryptocurrency linked to Iran as part of escalating economic pressure on Tehran. Treasury Secretary Bessent vowed to 'follow the money' and target all financial lifelines tied to the regime.
contents:
## Treasury Seizes Massive Iranian Crypto Holdings

### Seizure Details
The US Treasury Department, working with digital currency company Tether, froze $344 million in cryptocurrency across two wallet addresses linked to Iran. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced: "We will follow the money that Tehran desperately attempts to move outside of the country and target all financial lifelines tied to the regime."

### Evidence and Attribution
US government blockchain analytics experts observed "evidence of material links to the Iranian regime, including confirmed transactions with Iranian exchanges and a series of transactions routed through intermediary addresses that interact with Central Bank of Iran-associated wallets."

### Additional Sanctions
Following the crypto seizure, the Treasury Department imposed sanctions on a China-based independent teapot refinery for purchasing billions in Iranian petroleum, escalating enforcement of secondary sanctions on entities enabling Iranian oil trade.

### Iranian Crypto Activity
Cryptocurrency holdings in Iran reached $7.8 billion in 2025, according to crypto-tracing firm Chainalysis. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps accounted for roughly half of those holdings, "mirroring its dominance in Iran's economy more broadly."

### Expert Assessment
Atlantic Council senior fellow Daniel Tannebaum called the freeze "meaningful" but noted that Iran, "truly sanctioned out" after decades of restrictions, has adapted and put mechanisms in place to continue operating. The key pressure point, he suggested, lies with "third country actors enabling them," particularly China.
